text: Neuroscience has historically served as a vital wellspring for breakthroughs in artificial intelligence. We argue that to drive the next wave of AI innovation, we must prioritize fundamental research in "NeuroAI." A cornerstone of this initiative is the embodied Turing test, which moves beyond traditional benchmarks like chess or language to challenge AI models to navigate the physical world with the agility and sensorimotor sophistication of living animals. By shifting our focus from uniquely human cognitive feats to the robust capabilities shared by all animals—skills honed over 500 million years of evolution—we can address the fragility of current AI systems. Developing models capable of passing the embodied Turing test will provide a powerful roadmap for creating the next generation of intelligent, flexible, and energy-efficient autonomous agents.
